scope: contract / source boundary only; no broad framework yet

source_truth.head: c9857ee5
source_truth.canonical_build_spec_in_source: skaffold.yaml (skaffold/v3) declares 11 services in `app` config + loadgenerator in its own dependent config; cartservice has nested context src/cartservice/src

third_party_unchanged: redis:alpine (cart database; chart default `cartDatabase.inClusterRedis.publicRepository: true` keeps Docker Hub reference)

local_registry_target_proposal.rationale: Mirrors the existing AMOF platform substrate convention (host.k3d.internal:5000/amof-controlplane and friends). The `/gmd` namespace prefix keeps gmd-app images cleanly separated from AMOF platform images in the same local registry.

tag_convention_proposal.rationale: Matches the lifecycle build_lifecycle_build_command tag pattern (uses image_tag from request body, default dev-YYYYMMDD-HHMM).

chart_integration_points.redis_unaffected: cartDatabase.inClusterRedis.publicRepository defaults true; redis image stays on Docker Hub `redis:alpine@sha256:...`
templates_using_images_repository: templates/cartservice.yaml:257 (redis fallback when publicRepository=false; out of scope for first-party switch)
applicationset_integration_options: Option A (smallest): edit infrastructure/gitops/gmd/chart/values.yaml in place to point at host.k3d.internal:5000/gmd. Affects all environments equally (dev/test/prod). Simplest to land.
Option B (per-env): add `helm.parameters` to applicationset.yaml template `[{name: images.repository, value: ...}, {name: images.tag, value: ...}]` so dev can flip independently of test/prod.
Option C (per-env values files): add `infrastructure/gitops/gmd/chart/values-dev.yaml` referenced via `helm.valueFiles`. More structure, more surface area.
chart_integration_points.recommended_for_dev_only_first_proof: Option B (per-env helm.parameters in applicationset.yaml for the `dev` element only) so the boundary is environment-scoped and reversible without touching the canonical chart values. Option A is fine if the goal is to wipe upstream entirely from the local k3d substrate.

no_implementation_in_this_slice.rationale: Per task scope: contract/source-boundary only. Implementation is gated to a follow-up slice once the boundary is approved.

what_to_implement: Add scripts/build-gmd-app.sh that takes (--service <name>, --tag <tag>, --no-push) and builds ONE service (frontend) using the canonical Dockerfile at repos/gmd-app/src/frontend/Dockerfile, tagged host.k3d.internal:5000/gmd/frontend:<tag>
Re-use existing local registry mirror conventions and the cluster builder substrate proven by UP8-7..UP8-12; do NOT introduce a new builder
Wire one new command_builder.py entry build_gmd_app_lifecycle_build_command(root, image_tag, services=['frontend'], no_push=True) that returns (argv, cwd) targeting the new script
Dispatch in release_lifecycle_action() when name == 'gmd' and action == 'build'; do not yet flip the gmd contract — keep the dispatch path inactive at the API level
Run the script directly on host once to prove the frontend builds and the image lands in localhost:5000/gmd/frontend:<tag>
what_to_explicitly_defer: Flipping _ecosystem_build_contract('gmd') from mirror_upstream to source_build (would unhide Build button before the build path is complete)

Mirroring per-language base images (golang:1.26.1-alpine, mcr.microsoft.com/dotnet/sdk, etc.) for the cluster builder pod — host-only build is enough for the PoC; cluster-builder hardening for gmd-app is a separate later slice
exit_criteria: host.k3d.internal:5000/gmd/frontend:<tag> exists in the local registry catalog
scripts/build-gmd-app.sh exists and is executable on host
command_builder.py has build_gmd_app_lifecycle_build_command (unit-testable but not yet routed)
No live behavior change for gmd in the dashboard
